achievements.description: GDCU-8912 Cosmo Spins desktop 'rotate device' message removed (Harden Thomas Wallace, Done 10:27 BST) — desktop users no longer see the mobile-only rotation prompt on gambling.com/games/cosmo-spins
GDCU-8854 Geo selector arrow collapse bug fixed (Harden Thomas Wallace, Done 10:30 BST) — the arrow button in the Geo selector now correctly collapses the dropdown on repeat click

GDCU-7363 Slots widget minor filter issues resolved (Novica Vukobratovic, Done 11:30 BST) — pointer cursor on filter selection, 'Clear All' styling, and blue selection state now consistent with strategy widget

GDCU-8895 US Prediction Market page added to sitemap (Sanja Budic, High, Done 14:11 BST) — Prediction header on US sitemap, /us/prediction-markets linked, Prediction Reviews logic in place, correctly NOT under any 'Betting' headers. This was a morning high-priority action item.

GDCU-8611 'Feature' and 'Opinion' tags updated on News pages (Novica Vukobratovic, Done 14:29 BST) — labels now match updated Figma designs across news listing, article, category pages and all modules/widgets

GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts integrated (Harden Thomas Wallace, Medium, Done 14:46 BST) — GDC site detects and renders Genesis-prefixed CSS column layout classes (25/75, 50/50, 75/25, auto) with up to 3 nesting levels and mobile stacking

GDCU-8877 Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages moved to In Product (Novica Vukobratovic, 16:21 BST) — admin panel toggle for phone number field on /e and /p pages delivered for product review; stakeholders Corey Cunningham, Chloe Christie, Eoin McCarthy
Chloe Christie posted agenda links in #core-product and #6amigos for tomorrow's Product Sync and Mid Week Catchup — both Wednesday 06/05 meetings now confirmed with agendas

Gabriel Cornoiu added Standard.co.uk (Evening Standard/Indy group) new partnership to tomorrow's Midweek Catchup agenda — tech/product setup work (codebase + AdTech dashboard) imminent; Eoin McCarthy to create tickets

Gabriel Cornoiu confirmed in #6amigos (16:39 BST): Genesis will release the Brands Carousel component Thursday morning ahead of the weekly release — relevant to GDCU-8861 (Brand Carousel Integration, currently In QA with Harden Thomas Wallace)

Ciara Carroll published company-wide Claude Design etiquette and usage guidelines across #gdc-pod and #core-product — covering team rules, Figma relationship, GitHub integration requirements, and GDC design system access (gamblingcom-brand.netlify.app)

meeting_summaries.title: No confirmed meetings today (M365 calendar unavailable)
meeting_summaries.summary: The morning report contained no calendar data — Microsoft 365 authentication was not completed (OAuth flow not finalised). No GDC pod meeting notes were found in Confluence for 05/05/2026. The PubTech Dev Lead Sync (WK19) took place today per Confluence but is a PubTech-internal engineering meeting not within GDC product pod scope.
decisions: Genesis Brands Carousel component release confirmed for Thursday morning ahead of weekly release (Gabriel Cornoiu, #6amigos 16:39 BST)

action_points.action: ESCALATE — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll) is Blocked for Day 20 (since 16/04/2026). No movement today. Michael Butlin is listed as stakeholder and identified as the gate per Chloe (29/04). Michael Butlin is attending tomorrow's Product Sync — use that as the opportunity to unblock him directly in the room.

REVIEW — GDCU-8877 Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages (Novica Vukobratovic, Medium) just moved to In Product at 16:21 BST today. Review admin panel toggle for phone number field on /e and /p landing pages. Stakeholders: Corey Cunningham, Chloe Christie, Eoin McCarthy. Sign off or return feedback.

RESPOND — Jessica Dordevic DM (30/04 15:05 BST) re GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners is still outstanding after 5+ days including the bank holiday. Jessica is waiting for Mike's sign-off on the banners before she can proceed. Resolve first thing tomorrow.

DECISION — GDCU-8787 Carousel Banner Shortcode design direction (Joshua Smith, pending since 01/05). Option A: reuse Simple Banner shortcode + navigation arrows (Joshua's preference — reduces editor complexity). Option B: Free Games banner style (different layout, cannot reuse shortcode). Reply in #gdc-product-design-pod or DM Joshua.

PRODUCT SYNC PREP — Add Mike's agenda points to tomorrow's Product Sync before the meeting. Currently only one item on the agenda (Chloe on WC requests). Suggested additions: GDCU-8619 blocker with Michael Butlin, World Cup priorities (FTP WC game, Cosmo reskin, Group Bets), Vic Dadson return and data engineering reqs, GDCU-8546 Tinder widget blocked status.

MIDWEEK CATCHUP PREP — Review tomorrow's Mid Week Catchup agenda before the meeting. Key items: (1) Standard.co.uk new partnership — tech/product setup scope; (2) GDCU-8933/8934 AB tests — Chloe asking about dev feasibility and UX support; (3) Tinder widget now 'blocked' — understand the specific blocker; (4) Bloomreach push notifications moving to next sprint.

SIGN OFF — GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, Medium, In Product since 28/04 — Day 8). Still awaiting Mike's review. Approving this unblocks Carlos Blauth on GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to Learning Hub). Clarify with Ciara whether any outstanding feedback remains before signing off.

CHECK IN — Vic Dadson was expected back today from ~2 weeks sick leave (signed off ~21/04). No new Slack messages from Vic observed today. If check-in did not happen today, prioritise a brief call tomorrow: wellbeing first, then data engineering discussion (speak to Nemanja for clear reqs + knowledge sharing). Review her open backlog before the call.

TINDER WIDGET STATUS — GDCU-8546 Tinder Style Offer Match is now described as 'blocked for the moment' in Chloe's section of tomorrow's Midweek Catchup agenda. Previously it was In Product awaiting Mike's sign-off. Clarify the specific blocker with Chloe and Jessica at tomorrow's Midweek Catchup.

WORLD CUP PRIORITIES — Chloe's Product Sync agenda item asks about WC work in the pipeline beyond what's been shared with Mike. Prepare a clear list: FTP WC game (ticket to create), Cosmo WC reskin, Group Bets. Mike flagged these to Chloe on 29/04 but no tickets confirmed yet. Bring to tomorrow's Product Sync.

GENESIS BRANDS CAROUSEL — Confirm GDCU-8861 (Brand Carousel Integration, Harden Thomas Wallace) QA status ahead of Thursday's Genesis release. Gabriel confirmed in #6amigos (16:39 BST): 'Genesis will release the Brands carousel component on Thursday morning, just before our weekly release.' Verify QA sign-off on track with Robin John / Shane McGrath.

NEW PARTNERSHIP AWARENESS — Standard.co.uk (Evening Standard/Indy group) integration is imminent per Gabriel's Midweek Catchup item. Tech needs: codebase setup + AdTech dashboard. Eoin McCarthy to create tickets. Design team and matrix likely involved. Be prepared to discuss scope and design input requirements at tomorrow's Midweek Catchup.

DM GABRIEL — Intake form questions from 23/04 remain unresolved: (1) SEO considerations & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs, (2) for operator offers, which attributes to display. Also unresolved: Chloe Christie's intake form feedback (schema sub-section under SEO field, new page option with dropdown for editable content blocks). Confirm updated intake form is shared back.

MONITOR — GDCU-8847 Rev Share VIP Operator List (Nebojsa Stanic, UK, In Progress) updated today 09:34 BST. Active development — UK casino users with VIP ktag level 2+ to see custom operator list. Will likely arrive in In Product soon. Stakeholders: Gabriel Cornoiu, Chloe Christie, Joshua Smith.

MONITOR — GDCU-8806 In-App Age Gate 18+ Verification (Carlos Blauth, In Progress) updated today 12:45 BST. Compliance requirement for F2P mobile app (Apple/Google Play guidelines). No action needed yet but will arrive in In Product soon.

calendar._note: Microsoft 365 authentication not completed — exact meeting times unknown. Both meetings below confirmed via Slack and Confluence. Check calendar for start times. M365 OAuth initiated but callback URL not received — complete authentication to restore calendar data in future reports.

Have a clear position on the Tinder widget blocker — is it design, dev, or a strategic hold? Be ready to discuss Standard.co.uk integration scope and what product/design involvement looks like from the GDC side.
